name: Art Deco
summary: A precise, glamorous style using symmetry, geometric framing, refined contrast, and restrained metallic accents.

palette: ivory, black, deep jewel tones, and controlled gold accents
typography: elegant display headings with clean readable body text
surfaces: flat premium panels with framed edges
shapes: chevrons, rays, stepped geometry, and symmetrical borders
effects: crisp contrast and subtle metallic emphasis
layout: centered hierarchy, mirrored balance, and formal section rhythm
imagery: architectural crops, refined product staging, and geometric motifs

notes: Use ornament sparingly for business sites.
Prefer one strong motif per section.
Use borders and type treatment before heavy decoration.

recommendations: Position as elegant, precise, and premium.
avoid: Do not imply ornate luxury if the product needs plain utility.

recommendations: Use dark neutrals, ivory backgrounds, and a small metallic accent role.
avoid: Avoid large gold fills that reduce readability.

recommendations: Pair a refined display heading with a restrained sans-serif body.
Use framed or angular button treatments with high contrast.
avoid: Avoid pill buttons unless softened by brand constraints.

recommendations: Use symmetrical cards with crisp borders and reserved accent rules.
Use centered or formally balanced navigation.
Use structured columns and restrained dividers.
avoid: Avoid decorative overload at the page end.

recommendations: Add framed edges and disciplined spacing.
avoid: Avoid noisy pattern fills behind text.

recommendations: Use crisp frame, centered title, and calm entrance.
Use poised fades and short vertical reveals.
Increase border contrast or reveal a thin accent rule.
Use shallow depth and framed panels.
Use refined inline feedback with clear contrast.

accessibilityRisks.risk: Metallic accents can lower contrast when used as text.
accessibilityRisks.mitigation: Reserve metallic tones for borders, icons, and small accents.

css: Use border rules, high contrast, and restrained accent colors.
avoid: Do not copy historic ornament into dense interface controls.
